model::the llmwikidoc tool is configured.statement: The llmwikidoc tool is configured to use 'gemini-2.5-flash' as its model.

wiki_dir::the llmwikidoc tool is configu.statement: The llmwikidoc tool is configured to store its wiki documentation in the 'wiki' directory.

context_depth::the llmwikidoc tool is co.statement: The llmwikidoc tool is configured with a context depth of 2.

initial_confidence::the initial confiden.statement: The initial confidence score for llmwikidoc is set to 0.7.

reinforce_delta::confidence scores in ll.statement: Confidence scores in llmwikidoc increase by 0.1 upon reinforcement.

contradict_delta::confidence scores in l.statement: Confidence scores in llmwikidoc decrease by 0.2 upon contradiction.

decay_days::confidence scores in llmwiki.statement: Confidence scores in llmwikidoc decay over 30 days.

bm25_weight::the bm25 search algorithm h.statement: The BM25 search algorithm has a weight of 0.33 in llmwikidoc's search configuration.

vector_weight::the vector search algorit.statement: The vector search algorithm has a weight of 0.33 in llmwikidoc's search configuration.

graph_weight::the graph search algorithm.statement: The graph search algorithm has a weight of 0.34 in llmwikidoc's search configuration.

github (ssot)::github is the single sour.statement: GitHub is the Single Source of Truth (SSOT) for the distributed skill catalog.

soluciones ag\u00e9nticas tradicionales:.statement: Traditional agentic solutions suffer from architectural rigidity, increasing operational costs and regression risks.

catalog.yaml::the system centralizes cap.statement: The system centralizes capability discovery through a lightweight root catalog file (catalog.yaml) hosted on GitHub.

plataforma ag\u00e9ntica desacoplada::de.statement: Detailed technical logic and parameter schemas are delegated to skill folders using Anthropic Tool Standard (skill.json) and SKILL.md.

plataforma ag\u00e9ntica desacoplada::th.statement: The system implements a hybrid discovery pattern to hot-search and inject skills at runtime securely via Sandboxing.

catalog.yaml::catalog.yaml is located at.statement: catalog.yaml is located at the root of the repository and contains first-level discovery metadata and analytical tags.

skill folder::each skill folder must con.statement: Each skill folder must contain a skill.json and a SKILL.md file.

skill.json::skill.json follows the anthr.statement: skill.json follows the Anthropic Tool Definition Schema and defines input contracts for the LLM.

skill.md::skill.md provides cognitive an.statement: SKILL.md provides cognitive and governance guidance, business constraints, and few-shot prompting examples.

coordinating agent::if the coordinating.statement: If the Coordinating Agent calculates a local confidence score below 0.72, it delegates the task to the Catalog Explorer Agent.

sistema ag\u00e9ntica general basado en.statement: The system extracts 1 to 3 IT/Business tags from the user's request.
sistema ag\u00e9ntica general basado en.entity: Sistema Agéntica General basado en Skills Distribuidos

catalog explorer agent::the catalog expl.statement: The Catalog Explorer Agent downloads the raw catalog.yaml from GitHub's content API.
The Catalog Explorer Agent discards skill nodes whose tags do not intersect with the user's request.

catalog explorer agent::upon identifying.statement: Upon identifying suitable skills, only the respective skill.json is downloaded and dynamically injected into the Coordinating Agent's context.

sistema ag\u00e9ntica general basado en.statement: After a successful hot-discovered skill execution, the system asynchronously indexes its metadata into the orchestrator's local vector cache.
sistema ag\u00e9ntica general basado en.entity: Sistema Agéntica General basado en Skills Distribuidos

coordinating agent::the coordinating age.statement: The Coordinating Agent must be capable of orchestrating sequential multi-skill workflows based on a single broad user prompt.

motor de ejecuci\u00f3n::the execution e.statement: The execution engine processes LLM-validated payloads under three strictly regulated channels.

canal webassembly sandbox (c\u00f3mputo).statement: Canal WebAssembly Sandbox (Cómputo) is mandatory for dynamic code and mathematical calculations, runs Extism/Wasmtime within Python, has no network access, and uses isolated memory.

canal deno sandbox (i/o & apis)::canal d.statement: Canal Deno Sandbox (I/O & APIs) is a secure environment for TypeScript/JavaScript scripts, invoked via subprocesses with strict network flags and 5000ms timeouts.

canal mcp (model context protocol)::cana.statement: Canal MCP (Model Context Protocol) is for native bidirectional integration with corporate relational and vector databases via secure RPC.

canal docker (evoluci\u00f3n futura)::ca.statement: Canal Docker (Evolución Futura) is architecturally prepared for adding isolated ephemeral container execution in Oracle OKE.

motor de ejecuci\u00f3n::each agentic li.statement: Each agentic lifecycle must be persisted integrally and immutably for ISO 27001 / BASC compliance.

motor de ejecuci\u00f3n::the execution e.statement: The execution engine intercepts LLM-generated payloads, validates them against the skill's input_schema, and blocks execution if a mandatory parameter is missing.

sistema ag\u00e9ntica general basado en.statement: Aggregated latency for on-demand skill discovery, including fallback, must be less than 2.5 seconds.
sistema ag\u00e9ntica general basado en.entity: Sistema Agéntica General basado en Skills Distribuidos

aiagentscrew::the project name is 'aiage.statement: The project name is 'AIAgentsCrew — Distributed Skills Agentic Platform'.

aiagentscrew::the platform is built on g.statement: The platform is built on Google AI ADK.

skills catalog::skills live in a github-.statement: Skills live in a GitHub-hosted catalog.

skills catalog::the catalog consists of.statement: The catalog consists of `catalog.yaml` and per-skill `skill.json` files.

cli::interaction with the platform happe.statement: Interaction with the platform happens via CLI.

catalogexplorer::`catalogexplorer` (`src.statement: `CatalogExplorer` (`src/catalog_explorer.py`) handles catalog fetching, tag filtering, and lazy-loading of `skill.json`.

catalogexplorer::tests for `catalogexplo.statement: Tests for `CatalogExplorer` hit the live GitHub repo `https://github.com/ianache/skills-catalog`.

skill.json::`skill.json` files follow th.statement: `skill.json` files follow the Anthropic Tool Definition Schema.

python 3.11::the core tech stack include.statement: The core tech stack includes Python 3.11 and Google AI ADK (`google-genai`).

deno sandbox::deno sandbox execution has.statement: Deno sandbox execution has a strict 5000ms timeout.

reli-01::`catalog.yaml` responses are tt.statement: `catalog.yaml` responses are TTL-cached in-memory for 5 minutes.

disc-01::the default confidence threshol.statement: The default confidence threshold for `DISC-01` is 0.72.

reli-02::`github_token` environment vari.statement: `GITHUB_TOKEN` environment variable supports authenticated GitHub fetches, providing a rate limit of 5000 requests/hour.

mode::the `mode` configuration parameter.statement: The `mode` configuration parameter is set to 'yolo'.

commit_docs::the `commit_docs` configura.statement: The `commit_docs` configuration parameter is set to `true`.

model_profile::the `model_profile` confi.statement: The `model_profile` configuration parameter is set to 'balanced'.

depth::the `depth` configuration paramet.statement: The `depth` configuration parameter is set to 'standard'.

parallelization::the `parallelization` c.statement: The `parallelization` configuration parameter is set to `true`.

aiagentscrew::the project aims to build.statement: The project aims to build the missing agent layer on top of CatalogExplorer.

roadmap build order decision::the delive.statement: The delivery order for the project is architecturally mandated, starting with DenoRunner and ending with a CLI.

aiagentscrew::each phase of the roadmap.statement: Each phase of the roadmap produces an independently testable artifact, with end-to-end wiring occurring in Phase 5.

phase 1: deno execution channel::phase 1.statement: Phase 1 focuses on building an isolated DenoRunner with timeout enforcement, process cleanup, and typed error results, without ADK dependency.

denorunner::the denorunner must enforce.statement: The DenoRunner must enforce a hard 5000ms timeout for skill execution.

denorunner::the denorunner is responsibl.statement: The DenoRunner is responsible for ensuring no zombie Deno processes remain after execution or crashes.

skillinjector::the skillinjector convert.statement: The SkillInjector converts a SkillDefinition into an ADK FunctionTool that performs JSON Schema validation on LLM-supplied parameters.

coordinating agent + two-pass routing::p.statement: Phase 3 establishes the CoordinatingAgent, which handles confidence-gated routing, tag extraction, and JSONL logging.

coordinatingagent::a prompt with confide.statement: A prompt with confidence less than 0.72 triggers tag extraction and routing to CatalogExplorer.

coordinatingagent::the confidence thresh.statement: The confidence threshold (e.g., 0.72) is read from configuration (env var or config file) and does not require code edits to change.

jsonl logging::every routing decision by.statement: Every routing decision by the CoordinatingAgent writes a JSONL record containing prompt hash, extracted tags, confidence score, routing decision, and matched skill name.

catalogexplorer integration + caching::p.statement: Phase 4 integrates the agent with the GitHub-hosted skill catalog, using in-memory caching, GITHUB_TOKEN support, and rate-limit protection.

ttl cache::a ttl cache ensures that a se.statement: A TTL cache ensures that a second catalog fetch within 5 minutes returns a cached result without a GitHub network call.

github_token::when github_token is set,.statement: When GITHUB_TOKEN is set, catalog fetches use authenticated requests, increasing the rate limit to 5000 requests/hour.

cli entry point + end-to-end validation:.statement: Phase 5 provides a CLI entry point that starts an interactive prompt session and displays a progress indicator during Deno execution.

cli shell::the cli must provide distinct.statement: The CLI must provide distinct, user-readable error messages for timeouts, validation failures, and execution errors, avoiding raw Python tracebacks.

cli entry point + end-to-end validation:.statement: At least one real TypeScript skill (evaluar-test-case or especificar_user_story) must complete successfully end-to-end.

project state::the project's current foc.statement: The project's current focus is Phase 1 — Deno Execution Channel.

windows subprocess cleanup decision::for.statement: For Windows 11, subprocess cleanup in Phase 1 must use 'taskkill /F /T /PID' because 'os.killpg' is unavailable.

proc.communicate preference decision::to.statement: To prevent pipe deadlock on large Deno stdout in Phase 1, 'proc.communicate()' must always be used instead of 'proc.wait()'.

skill injection bridge::dynamic callable.statement: Dynamic callable construction for ADK FunctionTool in Phase 2 has medium confidence and requires live ADK experimentation.

coordinating agent + two-pass routing::c.statement: Confidence score extraction in Phase 3 requires an explicit Gemini structured JSON output design.

phase 1: deno execution channel::deno re.statement: Deno redirect behavior with '--allow-net' in Phase 1 has low confidence and needs verification against Deno 2.6.7 changelog.

core value::the core value of the projec.statement: The core value of the project is that a user types any prompt and the right skill executes automatically, with dynamic discovery and execution.

project state::the project status is 're.statement: The project status is 'Ready to plan' for Phase 1.

project state::the last project activity.statement: The last project activity was on 2026-05-17, focused on roadmap creation and requirements mapping.

denorunner::denorunner invokes typescrip.statement: DenoRunner invokes TypeScript skill files via Deno.

denorunner::denorunner has a hard, non-c.statement: DenoRunner has a hard, non-configurable 5000ms timeout for Phase 1.

skill i/o protocol::python passes parame.statement: Python passes parameters to the Deno subprocess as JSON via stdin.

skill i/o protocol::typescript skills re.statement: TypeScript skills receive input by reading Deno.stdin and parsing JSON.

skill i/o protocol::skills return their.statement: Skills return their result as a single JSON object written to stdout.

skill i/o protocol::stderr is reserved e.statement: Stderr is reserved exclusively for error output.

skill i/o protocol::if stdout cannot be.statement: If stdout cannot be parsed as valid JSON, DenoRunner returns an `execution_error`.

permission model::the caller passes `--a.statement: The caller passes `--allow-net` domains explicitly to `DenoRunner.execute()` as a list of domain strings.

permission model::domain validation uses.statement: Domain validation uses the strict hostname regex `^[a-zA-Z0-9.-]+\.[a-zA-Z]{2,}$`.

permission model::an invalid domain stri.statement: An invalid domain string returns a `validation_failure` before a Deno subprocess is created.

permission model::the caller (phase 2 sk.statement: The caller (Phase 2 SkillInjector) is responsible for specifying all Deno permission flags.

result type design::typed results are mo.statement: Typed results are modeled as Pydantic models.

executionsuccess::executionsuccess conta.statement: ExecutionSuccess contains the parsed JSON from stdout.

result type design::errors are designed.statement: Errors are designed as a discriminated union.

denorunner.execute()::`denorunner.execut.statement: `DenoRunner.execute()` always returns one of `ExecutionSuccess | TimeoutError | ExecutionError | ValidationFailure`.

denorunner::tests for denorunner are loc.statement: Tests for DenoRunner are located at `tests/execution/test_deno_runner.py`.

tests/fixtures/skills/echo_skill.ts::`te.statement: `tests/fixtures/skills/echo_skill.ts` is used for timeout, success, and error tests without network dependency.

pytest::`pytest` and `pytest-asyncio` ar.statement: `pytest` and `pytest-asyncio` are already in the project's stack.

prd::the prd explicitly calls out 'aisla.statement: The PRD explicitly calls out 'aislamiento V8 con timeouts innegociables de 5000ms'.

tc-03::tc-03 requires the orchestrator t.statement: TC-03 requires the orchestrator to kill the process at the strict 5000ms timeout and return a controlled error.

denorunner::the `denorunner` is a greenf.statement: The `DenoRunner` is a greenfield Python 3.13 module.

skill i/o protocol::python passes parame.statement: Python passes parameters to the Deno subprocess as JSON written to `stdin`.

skill i/o protocol::typescript skills re.statement: TypeScript skills receive input by reading `Deno.stdin` and parsing JSON.

skill i/o protocol::`stderr` is reserved.statement: `Stderr` is reserved exclusively for error output.

skill i/o protocol::if `stdout` cannot b.statement: If `stdout` cannot be parsed as valid JSON, `DenoRunner` returns an `execution_error`.

permission model::domain validation uses.statement: Domain validation uses a strict hostname regex: `^[a-zA-Z0-9.-]+\.[a-zA-Z]{2,}$`.

permission model::an invalid domain stri.statement: An invalid domain string returns a `ValidationFailure` before the subprocess is created.

permission model::the caller is responsi.statement: The caller is responsible for specifying all Deno permission flags.

result type design::typed results are mo.statement: Typed results are modeled as Pydantic models (v2).

result type design::callers use `isinsta.statement: Callers use `isinstance()` checks for results, not `try/except`.

asyncio subprocess with timeout and clea.statement: Process group killing on Windows uses `taskkill /F /T /PID`.
asyncio subprocess with timeout and clea.entity: asyncio Subprocess with Timeout and Cleanup

denorunner::`asyncio.create_subprocess_e.statement: `asyncio.create_subprocess_exec` is used for spawning Deno processes.

denorunner.execute()::`asyncio.wait_for`.statement: `asyncio.wait_for` is used to enforce the timeout on `proc.communicate()`.

asyncio subprocess with timeout and clea.statement: `proc.kill()` alone only kills the direct Deno child, not its full process tree on Windows.
asyncio subprocess with timeout and clea.entity: asyncio Subprocess with Timeout and Cleanup

permission model::`deno 2`'s `--allow-ne.statement: `Deno 2`'s `--allow-net` flag accepts a comma-separated list of hostnames.

denorunner.execute()::the `--no-prompt`.statement: The `--no-prompt` Deno flag is used to suppress interactive permission prompts.

denorunner::`asyncio` (stdlib) is used f.statement: `asyncio` (stdlib) is used for subprocess creation and timeout.

result type design::`pydantic` (`>=2.12,.statement: `Pydantic` (`>=2.12,<3`) is used for result type models.

denorunner::`re` (stdlib) is used for do.statement: `re` (stdlib) is used for domain hostname regex validation.

denorunner::`json` (stdlib) is used to s.statement: `json` (stdlib) is used to serialize parameters and parse stdout.

denorunner::`subprocess` (stdlib) is use.statement: `subprocess` (stdlib) is used for the `taskkill` call on Windows.

tests/execution/test_deno_runner.py::`py.statement: `pytest` is the test runner for Phase 1.
`pytest-asyncio` is used for async test support with `asyncio_mode = "auto"`.

dynamic agentic platform::the project ut.statement: The project utilizes Google ADK for agentic capabilities.

denorunner::deno is employed for sandbox.statement: Deno is employed for sandboxed execution of skills.

confidence-based routing::confidence-bas.statement: Confidence-based routing is a core architectural mechanism.

lazy tool loading::skills are dynamicall.statement: Skills are dynamically and lazily loaded into the agent's context.

coordinatingagent::`coordinatingagent` i.statement: `CoordinatingAgent` interacts with Gemini for prompt processing and confidence/tag extraction.

coordinatingagent::the confidence thresh.statement: The confidence threshold for direct execution by `CoordinatingAgent` is 0.72.

skillinjector::`skillinjector` validates.statement: `SkillInjector` validates skill input parameters against the `input_schema`.

denorunner::`denorunner` enforces a hard.statement: `DenoRunner` enforces a hard timeout of 5000ms for skill execution.

denorunner::`denorunner` manages the `--.statement: `DenoRunner` manages the `--allow-net` flag for Deno subprocess permissions.

coordinatingagent::`coordinatingagent` i.statement: `CoordinatingAgent` is a newly planned component residing in `src/agent.py`.

skillinjector::`skillinjector` is a newl.statement: `SkillInjector` is a newly planned component residing in `src/skill_injector.py`.

denorunner::`denorunner` is a newly plan.statement: `DenoRunner` is a newly planned component residing in `src/deno_runner.py`.

main.py::`main.py` is a newly planned co.statement: `main.py` is a newly planned component for the CLI entry point.

two-pass agent execution::the two-pass a.statement: The Two-Pass Agent Execution pattern introduces 1-3 seconds of latency on a cache miss due to two LLM round-trips.

adk functiontool::the adk `functiontool`.statement: The ADK `FunctionTool`'s signature is derived from the `skill.json`'s `input_schema`.

denorunner::skill parameters are passed.statement: Skill parameters are passed to the Deno subprocess via stdin as JSON.

denorunner::skill execution results are.statement: Skill execution results are captured from the Deno subprocess's stdout.

cli-based agentic platform::the project'.statement: The project's domain is a CLI-based agentic platform with distributed skill discovery (dynamic tool marketplace).

cli-based agentic platform::the confiden.statement: The confidence in the research is MEDIUM.

prd::primary research sources include th.statement: Primary research sources include the project's own PRD and PROJECT.md.

google adk::secondary research sources i.statement: Secondary research sources include training knowledge of Google ADK, Anthropic Tool Schema, and Deno sandboxing patterns.

natural-language intent routing::natural.statement: Natural-language intent routing uses a confidence threshold of 0.72.

confidence threshold::the confidence thr.statement: The confidence threshold (0.72) gates when to trust local knowledge vs trigger CatalogExplorer.

catalog fetch and tag-based pre-filter::.statement: Catalog fetch and tag-based pre-filter uses catalog.yaml as a lightweight index.
Catalog fetch and tag-based pre-filter is already implemented in CatalogExplorer.

lazy-load skill definition (skill.json):.statement: Lazy-load skill definition (skill.json) fetches skill definitions from GitHub raw content.
Lazy-load skill definition (skill.json) is already implemented.

dynamic tool injection::dynamic tool inj.statement: Dynamic tool injection is the bridge between CatalogExplorer output and Coordinating Agent execution.
Dynamic tool injection is the missing piece per PROJECT.md.

json schema parameter validation (pre-ex.statement: JSON Schema parameter validation (pre-execution) is a hard block on missing required fields.
JSON Schema parameter validation (pre-execution) is REQ-08 in the PRD.
JSON Schema parameter validation (pre-execution) uses the jsonschema Python library.

deno sandbox execution::deno sandbox exe.statement: Deno sandbox execution runs TypeScript skills.
Deno sandbox execution uses subprocess with '--allow-net=<domain>', 5000ms timeout, and no file I/O.
Deno sandbox execution is an already decided approach.

execution timeout enforcement::execution.statement: Execution timeout enforcement applies a 5000ms hard kill on Deno subprocesses.
Execution timeout enforcement is acceptance criterion TC-03.
Execution timeout enforcement uses Python's subprocess.run(timeout=5).

cli entry point::cli entry point is the.statement: CLI entry point is the v1 interface.

end-to-end happy path (one skill)::end-t.statement: End-to-end happy path (one skill) is the v1 acceptance criterion.

end-to-end happy path (one skill)::targe.statement: Target skills for the end-to-end happy path include 'evaluar-test-case' or 'especificar_user_story'.

zero-redeploy skill addition::zero-redep.statement: Zero-redeploy skill addition means adding a skill to the GitHub catalog makes it available without touching agent code.

confidence-gated fallback routing::the c.statement: The confidence threshold of 0.72 is a key design parameter for Confidence-gated fallback routing.

tag-based semantic pre-filtering (in-mem.statement: Tag-based semantic pre-filtering (in-memory) involves 1-3 tags extracted from the prompt, intersected with catalog tags in Python code.
Tag-based semantic pre-filtering (in-memory) is already implemented.

catalog.yaml::catalog.yaml is approximat.statement: catalog.yaml is approximately 100 bytes per skill.

skill.md cognitive guide::skill.md cogni.statement: SKILL.md cognitive guide is injected alongside skill.json.
SKILL.md cognitive guide acts as the prompt engineering layer per skill.

execution channel routing by skill type:.statement: Execution channel routing by skill type will use Deno for TypeScript I/O skills.
execution channel routing by skill type:.entity: Execution channel routing by skill type

execution channel routing by skill type:.statement: Execution channel routing by skill type will use WebAssembly/Extism for compute skills.
execution channel routing by skill type:.entity: Execution channel routing by skill type

execution channel routing by skill type:.statement: Execution channel routing by skill type will use MCP for vector DB skills.
execution channel routing by skill type:.entity: Execution channel routing by skill type

execution channel routing by skill type:.statement: Only the Deno channel is needed for v1 of Execution channel routing by skill type.
execution channel routing by skill type:.entity: Execution channel routing by skill type

immutable audit log (full lifecycle)::im.statement: Immutable audit log (full lifecycle) is REQ-07.
Immutable audit log (full lifecycle) aligns with ISO 27001 / BASC.

web ui / fastapi endpoint::web ui / fast.statement: Web UI / FastAPI endpoint is planned for v2.

fastapi::fastapi is already in requireme.statement: FastAPI is already in requirements.txt for future use.

multi-skill dag chaining::multi-skill da.statement: Multi-skill DAG chaining is REQ-06 in the PRD.
Multi-skill DAG chaining is planned for v2.

webassembly/extism channel::webassembly/.statement: WebAssembly/Extism channel is planned for v2 for compute-heavy skills.

multi-user / team support::the catalog s.statement: The catalog schema is designed to be multi-user-compatible with namespacing (e.g., comsatel.agente.core).

real-time streaming output::real-time st.statement: Real-time streaming output is planned for v2 when building the web UI.

skill versioning and rollback::skill ver.statement: Skill versioning and rollback for v1 uses git tags on the skills-catalog repo.

automated skill publishing pipeline::aut.statement: Automated skill publishing pipeline for v1 uses manual PR to the skills-catalog repo.

adk::each skill injected as an adk tool.statement: Each skill injected as an ADK tool carries its full `skill.json` schema in the context window.

gemini context window::gemini models hav.statement: Gemini models have a fixed token budget shared between the system prompt, tool schemas, conversation history, and the user prompt.

gemini context window::the tool schema p.statement: The tool schema payload alone can consume 20-40k tokens for ~15-20 skills.

adk::adk does not automatically truncate.statement: ADK does not automatically truncate or paginate tools.

github api::the gemini api returns `400.statement: The Gemini API returns `400 INVALID_ARGUMENT` for requests with payloads that are too large or have too many tokens.

deno::when `asyncio.create_subprocess_ex.statement: When `asyncio.create_subprocess_exec()` launches Deno and times out, Deno's child processes are not killed, becoming orphaned.

process group termination::`asyncio.crea.statement: `asyncio.create_subprocess_exec()` with `start_new_session=True` on POSIX allows `os.killpg()` to terminate an entire process group.

windows job object::on windows, wrapping.statement: On Windows, wrapping subprocesses in a `Windows Job Object` is the only reliable approach for job-tree termination.

catalogexplorer::the 0.72 confidence thr.statement: The 0.72 confidence threshold for `CatalogExplorer` is calibrated against one specific Gemini model version.

agentic platform::confidence scores from.statement: Confidence scores from LLMs are not absolute probabilities, but relative logit-derived values dependent on training distribution.

github api::unauthenticated github api r.statement: Unauthenticated GitHub API requests are limited to 60 requests per hour per IP.

raw content endpoint (github)::the `raw..statement: The `raw.githubusercontent.com` endpoint has much higher limits and is CDN-backed compared to `api.github.com`.

github pat (personal access token)::a gi.statement: A GitHub PAT raises the GitHub API limit to 5000 requests per hour.

json schema::json schema's default behav.statement: JSON Schema's default behavior allows additional properties if `additionalProperties: false` is not explicitly set.

json schema::the anthropic tool definiti.statement: The Anthropic Tool Definition Schema does not mandate the `additionalProperties: false` flag for skill authors.

python::python 3.11 is the recommended r.statement: Python 3.11 is the recommended runtime version for this project.

google-adk::google-adk 1.33.0 is the rec.statement: google-adk 1.33.0 is the recommended agent framework version.

google-genai::google-genai 1.75.0 is the.statement: google-genai 1.75.0 is the recommended Gemini API client version.

google-genai::google-genai 2.x is a brea.statement: google-genai 2.x is a breaking major version not supported by google-adk 1.33.0.

deno::deno 2.x is recommended for typesc.statement: Deno 2.x is recommended for TypeScript skill execution.

httpx::httpx is a transitive dependency.statement: httpx is a transitive dependency of google-genai.

pyyaml::pyyaml is a transitive dependenc.statement: pyyaml is a transitive dependency of google-adk.

python-dotenv::python-dotenv is a transi.statement: python-dotenv is a transitive dependency of google-adk.

jsonschema::jsonschema is a transitive d.statement: jsonschema is a transitive dependency of google-adk.

llmagent::llmagent's `tools` parameter a.statement: LlmAgent's `tools` parameter accepts Callables, BaseTools, or BaseToolsets.

basetoolset::basetoolset.get_tools is ca.statement: BaseToolset.get_tools is called on every agent invocation.

deno subprocess invocation::deno subproc.statement: Deno subprocess invocation uses `--allow-net=<domain>` for network control.

proc.kill::proc.kill() is the correct ac.statement: proc.kill() is the correct action for Deno subprocess timeout.

asyncio::asyncio.run() should not be mix.statement: asyncio.run() should not be mixed inside an already-running event loop.

gemini_api_key::gemini_api_key should be.statement: GEMINI_API_KEY should be loaded from .env and .env must be in .gitignore.

yaml.safe_load::yaml.safe_load() should.statement: yaml.safe_load() should always be used over yaml.load().

anyio.move_on_after::anyio.move_on_after.statement: anyio.move_on_after() is the canonical timeout primitive within anyio contexts.

google-adk::google-adk 1.33.0 ships llma.statement: google-adk 1.33.0 ships LlmAgent, Runner, BaseToolset, FunctionTool, and InMemorySessionService.

google-adk::google-adk supports python 3.statement: google-adk supports Python 3.10-3.13, with 3.11 being the stability sweet spot.

google-genai::google-genai >=1.72 is the.statement: google-genai >=1.72 is the minimum compatible version with google-adk 1.33.0.

aiagentscrew::aiagentscrew is a personal.statement: AIAgentsCrew is a personal CLI tool.

aiagentscrew::the project uses a google.statement: The project uses a Google ADK-powered Gemini agent.

aiagentscrew::the architecture is built.statement: The architecture is built around a two-pass routing pattern.

google-genai::installing google-genai 2..statement: Installing google-genai 2.x silently breaks google-adk functionality.

deno 2.x::deno 2.6.7 is confirmed as the.statement: Deno 2.6.7 is confirmed as the installed version for skill execution.

basetoolset::basetoolset.get_tools() is.statement: BaseToolset.get_tools() is the correct ADK API for dynamic tool injection per invocation.

dynamic tool injection::dynamic tool inj.statement: Dynamic tool injection is the primary build target and critical missing piece for v1.

pipe deadlock mitigation::pipe deadlock.statement: Pipe deadlock is prevented by using `proc.communicate()` instead of `proc.wait()` for subprocess output.

zombie process prevention::zombie deno p.statement: Zombie Deno processes are prevented by implementing process group kill on timeout.

tool schema bloat mitigation::tool schem.statement: Tool schema bloat is mitigated by tag pre-filtering and a hard cap of 8-12 tools maximum.

github rate limit mitigation::github api.statement: GitHub API rate limiting is mitigated by using raw.githubusercontent.com, an in-memory TTL cache, and GITHUB_TOKEN support.

confidence threshold stability::confiden.statement: Confidence threshold drift is mitigated by pinning the Gemini model version and externalizing the threshold to configuration.

tag vocabulary constraint::tag extractio.statement: Tag extraction open vocabulary mismatch is mitigated by including the actual catalog tag list in the Pass 1 system prompt.

allow_net validation::`allow_net` flag i.statement: `allow_net` flag injection is prevented by validating the domain format against a regex before subprocess command construction.

denorunner::the denorunner module has ze.statement: The DenoRunner module has zero dependencies on Google ADK.

models/skill.py::the models/skill.py mod.statement: The models/skill.py module (CatalogManifest, CatalogSkill, SkillDefinition) must not take ADK or Deno dependencies.

cli::the cli entry point uses `asyncio.r.statement: The CLI entry point uses `asyncio.run()` and has minimal surface area.
